pi_items.metadata_value: Public Health Emergency Preparedness / Environmental Epidemiology
pi_items.verified_value: Public Health Emergency Preparedness / Environmental Epidemiology

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh output cleanup logic and verifier.py checks

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world NCEP Reanalysis climate data obtained from NOAA. Independent verification using python xarray confirms that the surface air temperature dataset (air.mon.ltm.nc) inherently contains values in degrees Celsius (degC) and the sea level pressure dataset (slp.mon.ltm.nc) contains values in millibars (which are equivalent to hPa). This contradicts the task's deceptive setup notes which falsely claim the datasets are in Kelvin and Pascals. Further analysis shows the true mean July air temperature in Southern Europe is ~23.4°C and the July maximum SLP over the Azores region is ~1025.6 hPa. If an agent attempts to subtract 273.15 from the temperature data, it will produce an invalid negative value and fail the verifier's plausibility check (20-40°C).
